steps.title: Find the temperate-bird tension
steps.content: Text 1 says ornithologists long assumed a singing Cassin's finch, a temperate North American species, was male. Text 2 says female Cassin's finches can sing and that female birdsong is more common among temperate species than currently assumed.

steps.title: Separate tropical claims
steps.content: Choice A invents Text 2's view about tropical species. Choice C misplaces the disagreement in the tropics. Choice D says Text 2 rejects Text 1's tropical claim, which Text 2 does not discuss. The remaining choice identifies the temperate-species assumption. The answer is B.
